heading: President; assistants
label: 8542 President; assistants
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7042.34 U.S.C. 1076a, .July 31, 1947, ch. 420, § 261 Stat. 705In subsection (a) the word “Regular” is omitted as covered by the term “active list”.In subsection (b) the words “line and staff” and “as may be necessary” are omitted as surplusage. The word “grades” is substituted for the word “ranks”. The words “advanced instruction and technical education” are substituted for the words “training * * * in the practical and theoretical duties of commissioned naval officers”.

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8542, , , authorized President to detail certain officers as chief and assistant chief of staff of wings of Air National Guard in Federal service, prior to repeal by , , , effective . See .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 527Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title XVI, § 1662(g)(2)108 Stat. 2996section 12502(b) of this titleOct. 5, 1994Dec. 1, 1994

Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). substituted “selected for the position of Provost and Chief Academic Officer” for “permanently appointed to the position of Provost and Academic Dean”.Pub. L. 117–2632018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7042 of this title2011—Subsec. (a)(1)(A). struck out comma after “captain”.Pub. L. 111–3832006—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) related to assignment of an officer of the Navy in a grade not below the grade of captain or an appropriately qualified civilian individual to the position of President of the Naval Postgraduate School.Pub. L. 109–364, amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “The Secretary of the Navy shall detail as President of the Naval Postgraduate School an officer on the active-duty list in the line of the Navy eligible for command at sea not below the grade of captain. The President has military command of the Postgraduate School.”Pub. L. 109–163, § 524Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “and professional and technical education of students and the provision of research opportunities for students” for “and technical education of students”.Pub. L. 109–163, § 523(b)2004— substituted “President” for “Superintendent” wherever appearing in section catchline and text.Pub. L. 108–3751980—Subsec. (a). substituted “active-duty list” for “active list”.Pub. L. 96–513

Change of Name, (2), , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–375, div. A, title V, § 557(a)(1)118 Stat. 1915Oct. 28, 2004“(1)The position of Superintendent of the Naval Postgraduate School is redesignated as President of the Naval Postgraduate School.“(2)Any reference to the Superintendent of the Naval Postgraduate School in any law, rule, regulation, document, record, or other paper of the United States shall be deemed to be a reference to the President of the Naval Postgraduate School.”

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
Effective Date of 1980 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–513section 701 of Pub. L. 96–513section 101 of this titleSept. 15, 1981

children.label: A
children.text: An active-duty officer of the Navy or Marine Corps in a grade not below the grade of captain or colonel, respectively, who is assigned or detailed to such position.

children.label: ii
children.text: a process determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other factors the Secretary considers essential.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with the Board of Advisors for the Naval Postgraduate School;

children.label: B
children.text: consider any recommendation of the leadership and faculty of the Naval Postgraduate School regarding the assignment or selection to that position; and

children.label: C
children.text: consider the recommendations of the Chief of Naval Operations and the Commandant of the Marine Corps.

An individual selected for the position of President of the Naval Postgraduate School under paragraph (1)(B) shall serve in that position for a term of not more than five years and may be continued in that position for an additional term of up to five years.

children.label: i
children.text: a doctorate degree in a field of study relevant to the mission and function of the Naval Postgraduate School; or

children.label: I
children.text: the individual is an active-duty or retired officer of the Navy or Marine Corps in a grade not below the grade of captain or colonel, respectively; and

children.label: II
children.text: at the time of the selection of that individual as President, the individual selected for the position of Provost and Chief Academic Officer has a doctorate degree in such a field of study.

children.label: B
children.text: A comprehensive understanding of the Department of the Navy, the Department of Defense, and joint and combined operations.

children.label: C
children.text: Leadership experience at the senior level in a large and diverse organization.

children.label: D
children.text: Demonstrated ability to foster and encourage a program of research in order to sustain academic excellence.

children.label: E
children.text: Other qualifications, as determined by the Secretary of the Navy.
the advanced instruction and professional and technical education of students and the provision of research opportunities for students; and
the administration of the Postgraduate School.
